text: The Artist table (Artist) contains the ArtistId (PK) and the Name (NVARCHAR 120).
The Album table (Album) contains AlbumId (PK), Title (NVARCHAR 160), and ArtistId (FK).
The Track table (Track) contains TrackId (PK), Name (NVARCHAR 200), and AlbumId (FK).
The Track table includes MediaTypeId (FK), GenreId (FK), and Composer (NVARCHAR 220).
The Track table defines Milliseconds (INT), Bytes (INT), and UnitPrice (NUMERIC 10,2).
The Genre table (Genre) consists of GenreId (PK) and Name (NVARCHAR 120).
The MediaType table (MediaType) consists of MediaTypeId (PK) and Name (NVARCHAR 120).
The Playlist table (Playlist) consists of PlaylistId (PK) and Name (NVARCHAR 120).
The PlaylistTrack table is a junction table containing PlaylistId (FK) and TrackId (FK).
The PlaylistTrack table uses a composite Primary Key consisting of (PlaylistId, TrackId).
The Invoice table (Invoice) contains InvoiceId (PK) and CustomerId (FK).
The Invoice table stores InvoiceDate (DATETIME) and BillingAddress (NVARCHAR 70).
The Invoice table contains BillingCity (NVARCHAR 40) and BillingState (NVARCHAR 40).
The Invoice table contains BillingCountry (NVARCHAR 40) and BillingPostalCode (NVARCHAR 10).
The Invoice table stores a calculated Total column of type NUMERIC 10,2.
The InvoiceLine table (InvoiceLine) contains InvoiceLineId (PK) and InvoiceId (FK).
The InvoiceLine table contains TrackId (FK), UnitPrice (NUMERIC 10,2), and Quantity (INT).
The Customer table (Customer) contains CustomerId (PK), FirstName (NVARCHAR 40), and LastName (NVARCHAR 20).
The Customer table stores Company (NVARCHAR 80), Address (NVARCHAR 70), and City (NVARCHAR 40).
The Customer table stores State (NVARCHAR 40), Country (NVARCHAR 40), and PostalCode (NVARCHAR 10).
The Customer table stores Phone (NVARCHAR 24), Fax (NVARCHAR 24), and Email (NVARCHAR 60).
The Customer table contains SupportRepId (FK) which references EmployeeId in the Employee table.
The Employee table (Employee) contains EmployeeId (PK), LastName (NVARCHAR 20), and FirstName (NVARCHAR 20).
The Employee table contains Title (NVARCHAR 30), ReportsTo (FK), and BirthDate (DATETIME).
The Employee table contains HireDate (DATETIME), Address (NVARCHAR 70), and City (NVARCHAR 40).
The Employee table contains State (NVARCHAR 40), Country (NVARCHAR 40), and PostalCode (NVARCHAR 10).
The Employee table contains Phone (NVARCHAR 24), Fax (NVARCHAR 24), and Email (NVARCHAR 60).
The Album.ArtistId foreign key references Artist.ArtistId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Track.AlbumId foreign key references Album.AlbumId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Track.GenreId foreign key references Genre.GenreId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Track.MediaTypeId foreign key references MediaType.MediaTypeId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Invoice.CustomerId foreign key references Customer.CustomerId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The InvoiceLine.InvoiceId foreign key references Invoice.InvoiceId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The InvoiceLine.TrackId foreign key references Track.TrackId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Customer.SupportRepId foreign key references Employee.EmployeeId with a Many-to-One relationship.
The Employee.ReportsTo foreign key is a self-referential key referencing Employee.EmployeeId.
The relationship between Artist and Track is an indirect Many-to-Many resolved through Album.
The relationship between Playlist and Track is a direct Many-to-Many resolved through PlaylistTrack.
The relationship between Invoice and Track is a direct Many-to-Many resolved through InvoiceLine.
The Employee table represents a hierarchical tree structure via the ReportsTo column.
All tables in the Chinook schema use Surrogate Keys (integers) as Primary Keys.
The NVARCHAR data type is used for all string columns to support Unicode characters.
The NUMERIC(10,2) type is used for all currency columns to ensure decimal precision.
The DATETIME type is used for all date-related columns, including BirthDate and InvoiceDate.
The GenreId column in the Track table is technically an optional field but populated in standard data.
The Composer column in the Track table allows for NULL values where authorship is unknown.
The BillingState column in the Invoice table allows for NULL values for countries without states.
The State column in the Customer table allows for NULL values for international customers.
The Company column in the Customer table is nullable for individual (non-corporate) customers.
The Fax column in both Employee and Customer tables is nullable and often empty.
The ReportsTo column in the Employee table is NULL for the top-level manager record.
The UnitPrice in the InvoiceLine table captures the price at the time of transaction.
The UnitPrice in the Track table represents the current catalog price of the item.
The Quantity column in InvoiceLine is an INTEGER and is almost always set to 1 in sample data.
The Title column in the Employee table is limited to 30 characters and describes job roles.
The Name column in the Genre table is limited to 120 characters.
The Name column in the MediaType table is limited to 120 characters.
The Title column in the Album table is limited to 160 characters.
The Name column in the Track table is limited to 200 characters.
The Name column in the Artist table is limited to 120 characters.
To join Artist to Track, the join path is Artist -> Album -> Track.
To join Genre to InvoiceLine, the join path is Genre -> Track -> InvoiceLine.
To join Employee to Invoice, the join path is Employee -> Customer -> Invoice.
To join MediaType to Playlist, the join path is MediaType -> Track -> PlaylistTrack -> Playlist.
The Artist table is a 'parent' table to the Album table.
The Album table is a 'parent' table to the Track table.
The Customer table is a 'parent' table to the Invoice table.
The Invoice table is a 'parent' table to the InvoiceLine table.
The Employee table is a 'parent' table to the Customer table through the SupportRepId.
The Genre table is a lookup table for the Track table.
The MediaType table is a lookup table for the Track table.
The PlaylistTrack table acts as a 'bridge' or 'link' table.
The primary key of the InvoiceLine table is an IDENTITY column named InvoiceLineId.
The primary key of the Invoice table is an IDENTITY column named InvoiceId.
The primary key of the Customer table is an IDENTITY column named CustomerId.
The primary key of the Employee table is an IDENTITY column named EmployeeId.
The primary key of the Track table is an IDENTITY column named TrackId.
The primary key of the Album table is an IDENTITY column named AlbumId.
The primary key of the Artist table is an IDENTITY column named ArtistId.
The primary key of the Genre table is an IDENTITY column named GenreId.
The primary key of the MediaType table is an IDENTITY column named MediaTypeId.
The primary key of the Playlist table is an IDENTITY column named PlaylistId.
A standard SQL JOIN between Album and Artist uses the condition 'Album.ArtistId = Artist.ArtistId'.
A standard SQL JOIN between Track and Album uses the condition 'Track.AlbumId = Album.AlbumId'.
A standard SQL JOIN between Invoice and Customer uses the condition 'Invoice.CustomerId = Customer.CustomerId'.
A standard SQL JOIN between InvoiceLine and Invoice uses the condition 'InvoiceLine.InvoiceId = Invoice.InvoiceId'.
A standard SQL JOIN between Customer and Employee uses the condition 'Customer.SupportRepId = Employee.EmployeeId'.
The 'total' sales for an artist requires summing InvoiceLine.UnitPrice * InvoiceLine.Quantity across the join path.
The Chinook schema follows 3rd Normal Form (3NF) to minimize data redundancy.
The schema supports a hierarchical 'Reporting' structure for employees through self-joins.
The Invoice table tracks the 'Billing' geography which may differ from the Customer's home geography.
Every Track record must have an associated AlbumId to be valid in the schema.
Every Album record must have an associated ArtistId to be valid in the schema.
Every Invoice record must have an associated CustomerId to be valid in the schema.
The UnitPrice in the Track table cannot be negative according to standard domain constraints.
The Bytes column in the Track table is used for sizing calculations and storage metrics.
The Milliseconds column in the Track table is used for duration and playlist length metrics.
The Chinook database can be represented as an Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) with 11 entities.
The schema was specifically designed for cross-database compatibility including SQLite, MySQL, and PostgreSQL.
All foreign key constraints in Chinook ensure referential integrity across the music retail store model.
